contact.contactDisclaimer: We receive a significant number of emails each day. If you’ve asked for a reply, we’ll aim to respond as soon as we can, although during parliamentary recesses it may take slightly longer for us to respond. We’re usually able to reply more quickly to emails than to post. Please note, the Committee does not look at individual cases or specific complaints.

news.heading: Government ‘flying blind’ on regional growth without proper data, MPs warn
news.teaser: Simply pouring money into the existing innovation ecosystem is not enough to facilitate economic growth, MPs warn today. A new report from the Science, Innovation and Technology Committee urges the Government to take a more rigorous, data-driven approach to assess innovation policy.

news.heading: Housing Committee examines Draft Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill and publishes written evidence submissions
news.teaser: On Tuesday, the Housing, Communities and Local Government (HCLG) Committeeexamines banning the sale of new leasehold flats and making commonhold the default tenure, as it continues its inquiry scrutinising the Government’s draft Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill.

news.heading: Student loans: new inquiry on repayment terms and the taxation of graduates launches
news.teaser: The Treasury Committee is launching a new inquiry into student loans and the broader taxation of graduates

news.heading: Decisions of the Petitions Committee, 10 March 2026
news.teaser: Decisions of the Petitions Committee, 10 March 2026.
news.imageAltText: Grand Committee Room Palace of Westminster. A horseshoe shaped table is in the foreground and rows of benches and seats are at the sides to the rear of the image.

news.heading: MPs will debate a petition relating to automatic by-elections following Member defections
news.teaser: On Monday 16 March, MPs will debate a petition relating to automatic by-elections following Member defections.
news.imageAltText: Palace of Westminster with Wesminster Bridge at sunset

news.heading: Independent Expert Panel to investigate pitfalls in young people’s transition to adult health and care services
news.teaser: The Health and Social Care Committee today announces that its Independent Expert Panel (IEP) will investigate to what extent young people are being let down when transitioning from children’s services to adult services in health and care.

news.heading: Report publication: Earned Settlement: Examining the Government’s proposed reforms
news.teaser: The Home Affairs Committee has published its report into the Government’s proposed reforms to settlement, also known as Indefinite Leave to Remain.

news.heading: IDC Chair: New report should end plans to scrap UK aid watchdog
news.teaser: The Chair of the International Development Committee has responded to a new review of the stewardship of UK aid spending.

news.heading: Appointment of Chair of the Office for Legal Complaints
news.teaser: The Justice Committee has published its report following the pre-appointment hearing on Wednesday 11 March

news.heading: Post Office Horizon IT scandal: “serious structural failings persist” in redress
news.teaser: One year on from the Committee’s last report on the Post Office Horizon scandal, progress has been made delivering redress for victims of the Fujitsu-supplied Horizon IT scandal. More than 11,300 claimants have received payments with £1.44 billion distributed.

news.heading: Charter Review: MPs launch inquiry on future purpose, governance and funding of BBC
news.teaser: The Culture, Media and Sport Committee is inviting written submissions on the future of the BBC as part of a new inquiry into the Charter Review.
